evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC layout directly addresses the brief; AUROC and AP annotated per method in legends; diagonal and prevalence baselines present.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none' set; PDF uses Type3/DejaVuSans (bitmap glyphs, non-selectable text); SVG text also converted to paths; default DejaVu Sans font used throughout.
evidence.palette: Four distinct colors with different linestyles aid discrimination; however Method-A=green (#6ACC65) and Method-B=red (#D65F5F) form a red-green pair problematic for deuteranopia/protanopia; linestyle redundancy partially mitigates this.
evidence.layout_density: Figure is 8.5x3.8 inches with equal-aspect square panels, tight_layout applied, no obvious whitespace waste or label overlap; y-range 0-1 is appropriate for both ROC and PRC.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and references data.json; legend fontsize 6.5pt is quite small and may be flagged; 'Chance' label in ROC legend is redundant with the dashed diagonal convention.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains only a Type3 DejaVuSans font (bitmap glyphs) with no selectable text and no pdf.fonttype=42 set; curves are vector paths but text is not editable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (19 KB), figure.png (108 KB), figure.svg (75 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies both panels, explains reference lines, states metric names (AUROC, AP), and makes a specific comparative claim ('OursModel achieves the highest AUROC and AP among all methods evaluated').

overall_strengths: Complete two-panel ROC+PRC layout with all required annotations (AUROC, AP, diagonal, prevalence baseline) directly fulfilling the brief.
Caption is specific and informative, naming the winning method with a clear comparative claim and referencing the data source.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' are not set, resulting in Type3 bitmap-glyph text in the PDF and path-converted text in the SVG — neither is editable or selectable.
Red-green color pair (Method-A / Method-B) is a colorblind accessibility concern, and legend font size (6.5pt) is below the recommended minimum (~8pt) for publication.
